title: phlomoides pulvinaris
intro: Phlomoides pulvinaris, also known as the desert lavender, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Phlomoides pulvinaris include reducing anxiety and stress, relieving respiratory issues, and aiding in wound healing, among others. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, tinctures, and infused oils that can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
